conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: When we started the cloud migration, I believed that AWS was the only viable provider for enterprise-grade infrastructure. Their ecosystem is just unmatched.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: AWS certainly has the dominant market share and the deepest catalog of services.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But their pricing complexity and data egress fees are predatory. I changed my stance completely last quarter and became a vocal advocate for moving everything to a multi-cloud setup spanning GCP and Azure to avoid vendor lock-in.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Multi-cloud can mitigate lock-in risks, though it introduces operational complexity.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: You're not wrong about the complexity. Managing Kubernetes clusters across two cloud giants doubled our DevOps overhead. My current, definitive belief is that bare-metal on-premise infrastructure paired with an open-source private cloud stack is the only way to achieve predictable performance and cost control.

query: What does the user believe is the ideal infrastructure deployment environment?
